evals.prompt: I've been working on fixing tmux session persistence on NixOS for the last hour. We decided to use a simple auto-setup script instead of resurrect because the store paths kept breaking. I created a new module and added it to the config. Can you take a snapshot of what we accomplished?
evals.expected_output: Creates snapshot directory with files for decisions (resurrect vs auto-script), modules_added (new tmux module), and summary of work done

evals.prompt: Just finished debugging that home-manager backup file issue. Turns out we needed to add force=true to the config files. I also discovered that NixOS rebuilds are really slow when you have lots of options. What should I snapshot from this session?
evals.expected_output: Captures fix (backup file handling), learning (NixOS rebuild performance), and any decisions made about the approach

evals.prompt: Today I added support for the snapshot skill itself - created the SKILL.md, test cases, and the main script. We're using a sessions/ subdirectory structure with dates. /snapshot
evals.expected_output: Creates snapshot with modules_added (snapshot skill files), decisions (directory structure approach), and todos (still need to test and iterate)
